# How to Control Smells & Odors in the Cannabis Grow Room
_Last updated Sep 21, 2025_
by Sirius Fourside, co-founder GrowWeedEasy.com
**Quick Summary:** Best way to stop weed smell is a _[carbon filter](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=carbon+filter&i=lawngarden&crid=XS42GFUTVCRJ&sprefix=carbon+filter%2Clawngarden%2C122&linkCode=ll2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=3ebc3e6fd301c6be0a70f7922b7d939d&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) in a [grow tent](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-grow-tent)_. [Example no-smell cannabis setup with grow light](https://www.amazon.com/MARS-HYDRO-2x4ft-Hydroponic-Ventilation/dp/B08QMKBCQ3?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=a774ea6f20f6c6716d8555332249cd7f&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (~10 oz harvest).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/08/statue-nose.jpg)
_What’s that smell?_
**Have you ever caught a hint of marijuana smell in the breeze?**
Even if you couldn’t spot the source, you wouldn’t doubt what your nose told you.
I love that pungent, unique marijuana smell. Unfortunately, this delightful aroma is instantly recognizable and can stir up trouble for a cannabis grower.
_Why?_ A grow room with flowering marijuana plants is a factory of that delicious tell-tale weed smell. To protect your girls, you must make smell a non-issue.
Here are the main ways to get rid of cannabis smell in your grow room:
  * [Carbon Filter/Scrubber](https://www.growweedeasy.com/smell#carbon-filter-carbon-scrubber) (Best)
  * [Odor Neutralizers](https://www.growweedeasy.com/smell#odor-neutralizers-cover-up-the-smell) – Chemical method (Avoid)
  * [Masking Agents](https://www.growweedeasy.com/smell#cover-up-weed-smell-oil-incense-candle-growweedeasy-no-smell-tutorial) – Natural oils & things like incense (A bit effective)
  * [Air Purifiers](https://www.growweedeasy.com/smell#air-purifiers-air-filters) – Works a little (Not the best)
  * [Ozone Generators – (DON’T USE!)](https://www.growweedeasy.com/smell#ozone-generator-DONT-GET)

**Best option: Carbon filter in a grow tent** , like this setup (apparatus behind the LED at top-back of tent) – [check out the grow journal](https://www.growweedeasy.com/sweet-dreams-photoperiod-grow-journal-in-progress)!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/watering-cannabis-grow-tent-with-carbon-filter-no-smells-growweedeasy-sm.jpg)
In today’s “no smells in the grow room” tutorial, get up to speed on the options available to neutralize smells made by growing cannabis plants, and learn which methods are effective to…
  * **Cover up weed smell** – Find a way to hide or cover up the smell. This can sometimes work for small plants, or a strain with a very [mild cannabis smell](https://www.growweedeasy.com/examples-of-low-odor-cannabis-strains).
  * **Filter the aroma (use filter to “scrub” weed smell from air)** – Use a proven method to “scrub” the smells out of the air, typically using a [carbon filter](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=carbon+filter+grow+tent&crid=VAVDK2HX6594&sprefix=carbon+filter+grow+tent%2Caps%2C182&linkCode=ll2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e21b615482141d0c6275351f77c13b40&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) in a grow tent. #1 most effective method.

If you think about it, there are really only two real options when it comes to stopping smells in the cannabis grow room so **no one can ever smell anything**. Either **hide** or actually **filter** out the smell.
**You could vent smelly air directly outside** , which keeps smell out of the house, but neighbors can still smell marijuana when they come near. It’s best to filter the weed smell from air first. We don’t cover venting in this no-smell tutorial, but [learn everything about stealthy venting here](https://www.growweedeasy.com/stealthy-exhaust-systems-for-indoor-plants).
For a small grow with just 1 or 2 plants, you can sometimes get away with covering up the smell.
For a larger grow, you need to “scrub” the air in some way. Carbon filtering is the best way to ensure you don’t leak _any_ weed smells to catch the attention of nosy neighbors.
[Example no-smell cannabis setup](https://www.amazon.com/MARS-HYDRO-2x4ft-Hydroponic-Ventilation/dp/B08QMKBCQ3?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=a774ea6f20f6c6716d8555332249cd7f&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (up to 11 oz harvest). Check out the [grow journal](https://www.growweedeasy.com/nebulas-auto-flowering-cannabis-grow-journal).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/autoflowering-cannabis-plants-harvest-the-gang-side-view-sm.jpg)
# 1. Carbon Filter/Scrubber (Scrub odors from air)
[](https://www.amazon.com/s?k=carbon+filter+grow+tent&crid=VAVDK2HX6594&sprefix=carbon+filter+grow+tent%2Caps%2C182&linkCode=ll2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e21b615482141d0c6275351f77c13b40&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)These are hands-down the best option for controlling marijuana grow room odors. Carbon filters (also called ‘carbon scrubbers’) will actually pull the smells out of the air, neutralizing any odors that pass through.
Carbon filters are what you need if it’s important to neutralize the air coming out of your exhaust. A good carbon filter will make sure you never accidentally leak the smell of cannabis out through a window into your neighborhood.
These devices use activated carbon to chemically absorb smells and other impurities from any air that is pushed through the filter.
With a carbon scrubber, you need to set up a situation where the smelly marijuana air is being forced through the filter. In other words, you need to use a fan to _push_ or (even better)  _pull_ air through the carbon filter.
The most common way to do this is to set up a carbon filter as a part of your exhaust system in a sealed grow tent, and use an exhaust fan pulling hot air away from your grow area. As hot air is pulled out and away from your plants, the carbon filter scrubs odors from the air on the way out.
Example of a carbon filter set up in a [grow tent](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-grow-tent) – this device scrubs _all_ smells from the air so odors never leak outside the tent
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/carbon-filter-scrubber-exhaust-system.jpg)
Carbon filters are…
  * highly effective
  * extremely easy to maintain once they’ve been set up
  * last a long time

## **How to Find& Install The Right Carbon Filter**
**1.) Identify the diameter of your exhaust**
Many standard air-cooled marijuana grow lights are fitted with a 6″ exhaust hole (listed as 6″ air-cooled hoods). For most small-scale growers using a tent, you will want to get everything in 6″: including fan, carbon filter, and ducting.
_Why 6 inches? 6″ fans are strong enough to exhaust the air from nearly any small-scale grow. Unlike 4″ fans which are often too weak for 4″ carbon filters & will not fit standard air-cooled grow lights. 8″ and bigger filters are generally for more commercial applications; they require extremely powerful (and expensive $$$) fans suitable to moving huge amounts of air from a large enclosed area, plus they can’t be hooked up directly to standard grow lights._
**2.) Match up CFM rating between fan and carbon filter**
Make sure the “CFM” rating of the carbon filter is the same or  _slightly higher_ than the one listed on your fan. This ensures maximum efficiency but also has an added security benefit: it makes sure your carbon filter is only taking in as much air as it can effective scrub for odors.
For example, if the carbon filter says “300 CFM”, your fan should be rated as 300 CFM _or just a little lower._ A 600 CFM fan would pull more air than the 300 CFM filter could deodorize.
_Recommended 6″ Carbon Filter setup_
Quiet option – For a smaller 2’x4′ grow tent (**quiet** fan), the [Vivosun 6 inch smart filtration kit](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-Filtration-Controller-Ventilation-Hydroponics/dp/B0CBRQKVVN?content-id=amzn1.sym.95e4d6bd-d93f-4ee1-9766-ff64f54d2f71%3Aamzn1.sym.95e4d6bd-d93f-4ee1-9766-ff64f54d2f71&crid=1RXSYPXKF85BX&cv_ct_cx=6%22%2Bcarbon%2Bfilter%2Bkit&keywords=6%22%2Bcarbon%2Bfilter%2Bkit&pd_rd_i=B0CBRQKVVN&pd_rd_r=6657346c-b270-46fe-8bb1-0200510742c6&pd_rd_w=ezm9F&pd_rd_wg=nR7uZ&pf_rd_p=95e4d6bd-d93f-4ee1-9766-ff64f54d2f71&pf_rd_r=SHCJCJ1RY0N3WGAJTE11&qid=1741040202&s=lawn-garden&sbo=RZvfv%2F%2FHxDF%2BO5021pAnSA%3D%3D&sprefix=6%2Bcarbon%2Bfilter%2Bkit%2Clawngarden%2C135&sr=1-1-6024b2a3-78e4-4fed-8fed-e1613be3bcce-spons&sp_csd=d2lkZ2V0TmFtZT1zcF9zZWFyY2hfdGhlbWF0aWM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=c72f9b64ea6cf4b9f98cb61ede15a731&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) works great.
[](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-Filtration-Controller-Ventilation-Hydroponics/dp/B0CBRQKVVN?content-id=amzn1.sym.95e4d6bd-d93f-4ee1-9766-ff64f54d2f71%3Aamzn1.sym.95e4d6bd-d93f-4ee1-9766-ff64f54d2f71&crid=1RXSYPXKF85BX&cv_ct_cx=6%22%2Bcarbon%2Bfilter%2Bkit&keywords=6%22%2Bcarbon%2Bfilter%2Bkit&pd_rd_i=B0CBRQKVVN&pd_rd_r=6657346c-b270-46fe-8bb1-0200510742c6&pd_rd_w=ezm9F&pd_rd_wg=nR7uZ&pf_rd_p=95e4d6bd-d93f-4ee1-9766-ff64f54d2f71&pf_rd_r=SHCJCJ1RY0N3WGAJTE11&qid=1741040202&s=lawn-garden&sbo=RZvfv%2F%2FHxDF%2BO5021pAnSA%3D%3D&sprefix=6%2Bcarbon%2Bfilter%2Bkit%2Clawngarden%2C135&sr=1-1-6024b2a3-78e4-4fed-8fed-e1613be3bcce-spons&sp_csd=d2lkZ2V0TmFtZT1zcF9zZWFyY2hfdGhlbWF0aWM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=9214c0367d9f2b1f141b48dbfd1e6483&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
High Yield option – This [Vivosun 6 Inch 390 CFM fan & carbon filter kit](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-Controller-Temperature-Humidity-Ventilation/dp/B07SG989NY?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=29ade09ba8576847a438aa50f0c6c22c&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) fits a bigger 4’x4′ grow tent (this has a **louder** but **more powerful** fan)
[](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-Controller-Temperature-Humidity-Ventilation/dp/B07SG989NY?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=29ade09ba8576847a438aa50f0c6c22c&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**3.) Install fan and filter into exhaust system**
Refer to the diagram below to see how a carbon filter should be set up in your exhaust system. The carbon scrubber being placed at the back of the exhaust system minimizes the risk of flowering cannabis smell escaping.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/12/hid-grow-light-mh-hps-exhaust-setup-diagram-simple.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/carbon-filter-scrubber-exhaust-system.jpg)
Learn more about setting up a grow light with an exhaust:  
<https://www.growweedeasy.com/hps-grow-lights-setup>
**Smell Emergency?**
One trick to clear a room of smell that I’ve learned will work in an emergency is to put an exhaust fan on top of a carbon filter, and place it wherever the smell is strongest. I’ve done this when I’m cooking cannabis in the oven (which can smell up the house) or if I’ve been smoking and suddenly realized I have guests coming. It looks like this:
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/08/carbon-filter-attached-fan-sitting-on-ground-control-smell.jpg)
You don’t even have to attach the fan to anything, you can just let the scrubbed air go back into the room. It’s pretty amazing how fast this can clear up a smelly area! I learned this from a dispensary owner. [Here is an example of a fan/carbon filter combo](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-Inline-Control-Australia-Charcoal/dp/B07B3LG55T/ref=as_li_ss_tl?keywords=6%22+carbon+filter&qid=1548187791&s=Home+Improvement&sr=1-5&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=cd15a92f7feff5a85c80820ccfa6fe9f&language=en_US).
# 2. Odor Neutralizers (Use chemical means to cover up the smell)
**Note: It is recommended you do not place these products in your grow room or tent with flowering cannabis plants. Strong odor neutralizers work so well they can actually reduce or alter the flavor of buds over time. However, these are incredibly effective at neutralizing odors outside the grow area.**
[](https://www.amazon.com/Ona-Products-ON10061-25-8-Neutralizer/dp/B00AC1N4DM?pd_rd_i=B00AC1N4DM&psc=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=929197f27ec8d0a1fe8d19b2e26a38e0&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)Unlike a carbon filter, odor neutralizers don’t “scrub” the smells from the air.
These products are designed to bind with unpleasant smells so that the smell is “neutralized”. Often they also have a very strong fragrance to help cover up smells.
I’ve heard of growers using everything from incense to “bathroom sprays” to cologne to try to cover up the smell of a flowering marijuana plant.
Most household products like wall plug-ins and sprays just aren’t strong enough to cover up the smell of a marijuana grow room for long, if at all.
Also, using these products on your cannabis plants can reduce or alter their natural smell.
Any odor neutralizer or fragrance needs to be constant since your plants don’t stop making their unique flowering scent once they start. Once the smell arrives in your grow room, it basically keeps getting stronger until harvest.
However, there are a few heavy-duty products available that are effective for small-scale marijuana grow rooms.
_Sprays, Glade Plugins, etc_ – Generally these are only effective for a few hours at most. Only recommended in an emergency!
Best spray to cover up smells in an emergency: [Ozium Air Sanitizer](https://www.amazon.com/Ozium-Sanitizer-Eliminator-Offices-Original/dp/B07YVS3DVM?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=f72c2d54121b52e6d55f760f480495f4&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) – this smells very “citrus clean” that seems to do pretty well at covering up cannabis odor without smelling like you just sprayed a bunch of Febreeze. The only thing to keep in mind is a little bit goes a long way. A quick spritz is all you need!
[](https://www.amazon.com/Ozium-Sanitizer-Eliminator-Offices-Original/dp/B07YVS3DVM?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=f72c2d54121b52e6d55f760f480495f4&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
I think the [vanilla version](https://www.amazon.com/Ozium-Sanitizer-Eliminator-Offices-Vanilla/dp/B097QKB713?dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.UdoWtR2Tc2A1J5v96GYrZNb0W8TEh3ChkeNT0BIM6fHHKTfIN_0llaLxgM7U_xJf3fI5r19VKfa8aQn5CFITDQOQQ_3cYaxaNLVYmSZewopWhWLAPuC29c1-dR_sudlverJu8C9OMfbcaVM3zIxe2tpT70UMV6JTWlXHJnJpRnS931YwDucDuffx4uJTJjkgmaeoM0KkzRH8WDBPBDNUnvzw06B84TcnpcLFBBo9mgP4ITJeztdWX39NGPgPwHh1GIZIaHah_MULHvW-OeO0n7g13LvV9LoATf1_8PrzYN4.L1dAq66GXyGroWqFjxtPOIHmUH-i71nLy4IUG06t-Bc&dib_tag=se&keywords=ozium%2Bvanilla&qid=1758398413&sr=8-6&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=1531e1068b35ae461d085d18f80b55a3&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) smells better, but a little goes a LONG way.
_Warning:_ Never spray any odor neutralizer near your plants in the flowering stage or the smell can stick to the buds!
**Recommended Marijuana Odor Neutralizer – Ona Products**
Ona Gel and Ona Blocks were originally developed to control the smell inside sewage facilities. They are now available to consumers because they are remarkably effective at neutralizing odors.
Ona products are quick and easy to use. They are effective at covering up smells in a small grow room, though they will only work for a relatively short window of time and must be replaced every few weeks up to a few months.
Ona Gel in the original scent seems to be the most effective for covering up marijuana smells in our tests. With a few flowering plants in a grow tent or closet, you will be able to cover up almost all smells with one or two of these placed outside the grow room.
**Never place Ona products in with your flowering plants!**
If you use Ona products in your living room, den, or other open areas, no one who enters those rooms will be able to smell any sign of your cannabis plants, even if the plants are just a room away.
But Ona products _can_ reduce or alter the smell of your cannabis buds if they share the same air, so you should never place Ona products in the same area as your flowering plants.
**If you need to neutralize the smell coming out of your exhaust** , a carbon filter ([#1 on this list](https://www.growweedeasy.com/smell#carbon-filter-carbon-scrubber)) is highly recommended. You could also have your exhaust air blow over an Ona product right before being vented outside. It is **not** recommended to use Ona products _in_ your grow area alongside your plants. Ona products are meant to be used _outside_ the grow area.
[PSA: Don’t use ONA to control smells IN the grow room](https://www.growweedeasy.com/ona-gel-cannabis)
How long do they last? It depends on the size Ona Product you buy, as well as the temperature/humidity of your grow area.
Generally, they seem to last at least 4-6 weeks for most growers, which is long enough for a few of these to provide coverage throughout the smelly second half of the flowering stage.
[**Ona Gel Pro**](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B002JLGMEG/ref=as_li_ss_til?tag=growwcom07-20&camp=0&creative=0&linkCode=as4&creativeASIN=B002JLGMEG&adid=0B1SVT7K5ZPG57Q4WSZW)  
[](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B002JLGMEG/ref=as_li_ss_til?tag=growwcom07-20&camp=0&creative=0&linkCode=as4&creativeASIN=B002JLGMEG&adid=0B1SVT7K5ZPG57Q4WSZW&)  
(stock up on these to make sure no one can smell cannabis in your home)
# 3. Masking Agents – Natural oils & things like incense (A bit effective)
These are the types of things you might already have around the house to improve/change the smell.
  * **Essential oils** – somewhat effective, but only can effectively cover up very small smells. Otherwise you just have the smell of weed mixed with the essential oil.
  * **Perfume / Cologne** – Even less effective, and it seems weird for a room to smell strongly of a personal scent, so not the best choice.
  * **Incense** – Usually more effective than essential oils, especially the really strong/smelly incense. “Patchouli” scented incense (and others) can be powerful stuff. The bigger issue with incense is that the smell of incense itself can be bothersome for some people, especially if you burn enough incense to cover up the smell of a cannabis grow operation.
  * **Scented candles** – Less effective than essential oils, typically. Usually you get a “weed plus the candle” kind of smell.

The strongest (and cheapest) incense is usually found at a head shop (bong shop) or local market. Online incense tends to smell much weaker.
[](https://www.amazon.com/Natural-Mint-Patchouli-Incense-Sticks/dp/B09DCD39RL?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=53a5deacd6a0a85174443b1269a621a1&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
# 4. Air Purifiers (Air Filters)
We all love the idea of having pure air to breathe. Many air purifiers come with the claim of reducing smells, usually by means of a filter. Do these work to get rid of smells in a marijuana grow room?
Not really.
It is true that some air purifiers do help remove _some_ smells from the air. However, these are not strong enough to pull _all_ the smells from a grow room.
A regular air purifier only removes a _little_ weed smell (marginally effective)
[](https://www.amazon.com/LEVOIT-Purifier-Home-Allergies-Pets/dp/B07VVK39F7?crid=OKSFE8AXC6C4&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.MJb10YmnoQ-TJscWQyvaPiuVS5rDKuznaJn09ScPvNOS5ZAbegK8Ub0URwMlwEwxemgex9CyVbwy2xaf_YKuU0udlCqnsGEijAXnC9QD1r-E0iglTEHb0RM_BgNDPP2ryOt3DQXBHy2-PSWMtlHvWxZsRo_C9DiRgg66lw37D1oQrGWcawOGrijMYNekPBzLCipkl-C4v9qAf0r1onVCaj9gIjypIVlvCf2YP1t7wqEn1SmDfWLFpqZJbovjB8Ev6ovBSYB1qcKey_-lVf0vcsvzY0BiKt6DZC6dCoTYQ8k.Ox4MXdVSaq3bTDplyheOSfnBotOB74Hxdtvs_CGQ5S8&dib_tag=se&keywords=air%2Bpurifier%2Bsmells&qid=1758397598&s=home-garden&sprefix=%2Cgarden%2C311&sr=1-5&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=ec8f6d0a3c80c7b2284ae342af51fa5a&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
Therefore, while air purifiers may be a good choice for other reasons, I do not recommend getting one just to try to get rid of smells in your cannabis grow room.
But it never hurts to keep one near the front door so everything always smells super fresh when you open your door.
I have the following ridiculously expensive air purifier, which I keep near the front door. I originally got it for allergies, which it helped with a lot (If anyone else struggles with allergies, it worked well enough I got a second one for the bedroom). But unlike other air filters I’ve tried, it actually does a pretty good job of removing weed smell, too.
Yet even this industrial air purifier has nothing on the cheapest carbon filter to get rid of weed smell!
[](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B08CPV2FP7?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=b40ca7e47af112bba385bf0af165bfca&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
# 5. Ozone Generator – DO NOT GET ONE!
Ozone generators are sometimes added to grow rooms to neutralize smells. Ozone generators claim to remove odors, clean the air, and kill mold and mildew.
Although some growers claim that ozone generators are the best means of eliminating odor, at GrowWeedEasy.com, **we strongly recommend against having one in your grow room.**
What’s the big deal?
Well, ignoring the fact that Ozone is not as effective as a carbon filter at reducing odors, one problem is that they’re bad for the environment. So much, in fact, that they’re illegal in the state of CA where I live (see: <http://www.arb.ca.gov/research/indoor/o3g-list.htm>).
More importantly, ozone generators are actually bad for **YOU!**
Here are just a few of the **many** links to research from reputable institutions all saying the same thing: Ozone generators are bad for humans!
  * [EPA (U.S. Environmental Protection Agency)](https://www.epa.gov/indoor-air-quality-iaq/ozone-generators-are-sold-air-cleaners)
    * “When inhaled, ozone can damage the lungs.”
  * [OSHA (U.S. Occupational Health & Safety Administration)](https://www.osha.gov/dts/osta/otm/otm_iii/otm_iii_2.html)
    * Ozone is listed as a “Major Indoor Air Contaminant”
  * [American Lung Association](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/05/residentalaircleanerfactsheet.pdf)
    * “Ozone is a potent lung irritant and exposure to elevated levels is a contributor to the exacerbation of lung disease”
  * [California Air Resources Board](http://www.arb.ca.gov/research/indoor/o3g-list.htm)
    * BANNED!
  * [Texas Department of State Health Services](https://web.archive.org/web/20180906155934/https://www.dshs.state.tx.us/iaq/SchoolsGuide.shtm)
    * “Ozone-generating devices should not be used in occupied spaces. Ozone is a lung irritant.”

Some growers claim to have safer ways to use ozone in their grows, such as venting ozone out of the house or making sure ozone only goes into the exhaust. Again, we would strongly recommend against using ozone since in the best case, it’s only doing what a carbon scrubber can do. In the worst case, it’s hurting the lungs of you, your pets and your family!
* * *
**Make Sure Only YOU Get To Enjoy Your Plant’s Smell!**
[](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/breeders/nirvana?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64)
**Start growing a Low Odor Marijuana Strain today!**
These strains produce neutral, non-marijuana-like odors… even as they approach harvest!
### 1.) Frisian Duck
[Frisian Duck](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/frisian-duck-feminised-seeds-dp-fdck-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) is the quintessential stealthy, low odor strain. Frisian Duck was designed from the beginning to be a “stealth strain” by the breeder Dutch Passion, who has continued to maintain and improve the strain. Frisian Duck gives you purple buds, duckfoot-shaped leaves, and a mild smell of spicy pine
On “duckfoot” strains like [Frisian Duck](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/frisian-duck-feminised-seeds-dp-fdck-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64), the leaves don’t look like regular cannabis leaves! Purple buds with a mild, spicy pine scent.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/frisian-duck-purple-cannabis-bud-cola-by-grower-eCa.jpg)
Where to get Frisian Duck seeds:
  * [North Atlantic Seed Co.](https://northatlanticseed.com/product/frisian-duck-f/ref/63951/) (Maine, US)
  * [Seedsman](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/frisian-duck-feminised-seeds-dp-fdck-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) (US, UK & Europe)

### 2.) Blue Mystic
Blue Mystic was originally designed by Nirvana and then Royal Queen Seeds took the mantle with their version. This strain tends to stay short, the buds get big, and the smell stays mild.
Where to get Blue Mystic marijuana seeds:
  * [North Atlantic Seed Co.](https://northatlanticseed.com/product/blue-mystic-f/ref/63951/) (Maine, US)
  * [Seed Supreme](https://seedsupreme.com/royal-queen-seeds-blue-mistic-feminised-seeds.html#grower) (California, US)

[Blue Mystic](https://northatlanticseed.com/product/blue-mystic-f/ref/63951/) is a famous low-odor marijuana strain that stays short and produces high quality buds.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/11/blue-mystic-auto-flower.jpg)
### 3.) Blue Sunset Sherbert
Buds that grow on Blue Sunset Sherbert plants by Barney’s Farm tends to have a lighter, sweeter smell than most cannabis buds, and lack the distinctive “skunk” odor.
Where to get Blue Sunset Sherbert cannabis seeds:
  * [North Atlantic Seed Co.](https://northatlanticseed.com/product/blue-sunset-sherbert/ref/63951/) (Maine, USA)
  * [Seedsman](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/blue-sunset-sherbert-feminised-seeds-bfs-blusunsherb-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) (US, UK & Europe)

[Blue Sherbert Sunset](https://northatlanticseed.com/product/blue-sunset-sherbert/ref/63951/) grows with a relatively low cannabis smell, and produces pretty, potent buds up to 28% THC.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/blue-sunset-sherbert-by-barneys-farm.jpg)
## Special mentions
Photoperiod strains
  * [Northern Lights](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/northern-light-feminised-seeds-rqs-nl-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) – The original “low odor” marijuana strain, with many legendary spinoffs.
  * [Master Kush](https://seedsupreme.com/master-kush-feminized.html#grower) – The classic marijuana strain known for big yields and “kushy” non-skunky smell.
  * [Jack Herer](https://seedsupreme.com/jack-herer-feminized.html#grower) – Unique smell doesn’t really smell like most other cannabis strains.

Auto-flowering strains – [what is that?](https://www.growweedeasy.com/autoflowering-vs-photoperiod/)
  * [Royal Runtz Auto](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/royal-runtz-auto-feminised-seeds-rqs-rruntz-auto-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) is an auto-flowering strain that is great for stealth growing due to its small size and relatively low smell (the buds still smell delicious). [Royal Queen Seeds autoflowering strains](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/cannabis-seed-breeders/dutch-passion-seeds?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) are all excellent in my experience, and relatively low smell compared to others.
  * [Northern Lights Auto](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/northern-light-auto-feminised-seeds-rqs-nla-auto-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) – Northern Lights power in autoflowering cannabis form, also developed by [Royal Queen Seeds](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/cannabis-seed-breeders/dutch-passion-seeds?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64).

[Northern Lights Auto](https://www.seedsman.com/us-en/northern-light-auto-feminised-seeds-rqs-nla-auto-fem?a_aid=536dc52ef2d64) has a surprisingly light scent.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/04/autoflowering-nirvana-northern-lights.jpg)

Notifications
